boundary_reason: Early Batman era begins with Detective Comics #27 and continues through the pre-Silver-Age period; exact end is less useful than title-specific boundary rows.
notes: Do not conflate with the whole DC Golden Age.

boundary_reason: Batman continues through the 1950s with science-fiction and lighter fantasy material; Library keeps this distinct from a clean post-1956 Silver Age label.
notes: Useful for future Silver Age/1950s cleanup.

boundary_reason: Julius Schwartz New Look begins in Detective Comics #327 and Batman #164, returning Batman toward detective-oriented stories.
Batman-specific Bronze convention begins with the late-1969/early-1970 status-quo shift and darker O'Neil/Adams direction; it ends at the post-Crisis Year One/Modern local handoff.
notes: This is a Batman-specific item convention, not a universal industry consensus.

boundary_reason: Crisis on Infinite Earths and immediate Batman issues bridge Bronze continuity into post-Crisis Year One continuity.
notes: Overlaps late Bronze and early Modern item rows deliberately.

boundary_reason: Existing Library collecting era begins around Batman #404 and Detective Comics #568 and runs through No Man's Land for current completion tracking.
